source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Contact Dermatitis

event.top_event: Contact Dermatitis from SOBM splash exposure
actions_taken: Helicopter evacuation of injured person on July 7, 2019
Review of work instructions and daily reports
Request for PPE documentation and POOH records
event.summary: On July 1, 2019, at 2:00 pm, during pulling of workstring (POOH) operations on the Ensco 102 rig at Vermilion Block 369, a floor hand was splashed with Synthetic Oil Based Mud (SOBM) on his legs. The injured person was not wearing required personal protective equipment (barrier cream, tyvek suit, nitrile gloves, face shield, and goggles). The SOBM soaked through his clothing, causing contact dermatitis with lesions, scabbed blisters, and signs of infection. The injured person continued working and did not report the injury until July 7, when he sought medical attention. He was evacuated by helicopter for onshore medical treatment. Investigation revealed inconsistent completion of Job Safety Analysis (JSA) forms, inadequate hazard identification in pre-tour safety meetings, and failure of supervision to enforce PPE requirements.

hazards.name: Splashing of hazardous fluids during POOH operations

threats.name: SOBM splash contact with skin due to inadequate PPE
Failure to identify SOBM hazard in pre-job safety meeting
Incomplete or inconsistent Job Safety Analysis documentation
Supervisor failure to enforce PPE requirements

consequences.name: Contact dermatitis with lesions and blisters on lower legs
Loss of work capability and mobility impairment

controls.name: Required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for SOBM handling
controls.barrier_role: Primary barrier to prevent skin contact with SOBM

supporting_text: The IP did not have the proper PPE when SOBM splashed on his clothing
At the time of the incident, the IP was not wearing barrier cream, tyvek suit, nitrile gloves, face shield and goggles or a mono shield
The SOBM soaked through his clothing contacting his legs causing a Contact Dermatitis rash

controls.name: Hazard Identification in Pre-Tour Safety Meeting
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify SOBM hazard and required PPE

supporting_text: The B-crew hazard identification prompt cards do not identify SOBM as a hazard and does not identify the correct PPE for working with SOBM
The work instructions and JSA did not identify SOBM as a hazard or include the required PPE
Inadequate pre-job safety meeting: Supervisors are not utilizing the JSA and signature page to identify the hazard of SOBM or required PPE

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) Documentation and Completion
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to document hazards, PPE, and job factors

supporting_text: The work instructions form for POOH with Job Safety Analysis (JSA) signature page with all job factors, barriers, and assignments was not completed
The supervisors on A&B crew are not completing the JSA each day for POOH furthermore; the JSA does not identify the PPE required for working with SOBM
BSEE investigators found the supervisors pre-tour safety meeting work instruction forms and JSA use is inconsistent

controls.name: Supervisor Enforcement of PPE and Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure PPE compliance through supervision

supporting_text: Not ensuring the use of required PPE: The supervisor on the drill floor did not use stop work authority when the IP was not wearing proper PPE for SOBM
Supervision: Not ensuring the use of required PPE

controls.name: Immediate Injury Reporting and Medical Attention
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to enable early detection and treatment of injury
controls.lod_basis: Early intervention to prevent infection and complications

supporting_text: The IP did not immediately report his July 1, injury. On July 7, the IP reported to the medic he was having trouble walking due to a skin rash he was self-medicating
Not following proper procedures: The IP did not immediately report his July 1, injury
The medic observed lesions and scabbed blisters to the front and back portions of both lower legs with open sores with signs of infection

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
